title: Veterinary Referrals Fort Lauderdale | Pet Emergency Center
description: After-hours emergency referral support for veterinary practices in Broward, Palm Beach, and Miami-Dade counties. Patients returned to your care. (954) 772-0420.
ogTitle: For Referring Veterinarians | Pet Emergency Center Fort Lauderdale
ogDescription: A trusted emergency partner for South Florida's daytime veterinary practices. We support your care, we return your patients, and we communicate every step.

mainEntity.name: Will my patient be returned to me for follow-up?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Unless the case requires specialty follow-up beyond our scope, we direct the owner back to your practice for recheck exams, suture removal, and ongoing care. Your relationship with the patient stays intact.

mainEntity.name: How do you send records back to my practice?

acceptedAnswer.text: Patient records are sent electronically to your practice email through our Idexx Neo practice management system. Case summaries are typically sent within 24 hours of discharge. If you need records sooner, call us and we can prioritize.

mainEntity.name: Do you handle exotic and unusual species?

acceptedAnswer.text: We see birds, reptiles, rabbits, and many exotic species. For very unusual cases, call us before sending the patient and we can confirm whether we are equipped to handle the situation.

mainEntity.name: What if the patient needs specialty care beyond your scope?

acceptedAnswer.text: We stabilize the patient, coordinate transfer to the appropriate specialist, and keep your practice informed. You will know where the patient went and what happened next.

mainEntity.name: Do you charge referring practices for case communication or records?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Case communication and records are part of how we practice, not a billable service.

mainEntity.name: What if I have a question after hours?

acceptedAnswer.text: Call Paige Obrig directly at (954) 288-5800 for after-hours coordination. During operating hours, the main line at (954) 772-0420 is the fastest path.

main:
For veterinary professionals

For Referring Veterinarians.

A trusted emergency referral partner for South Florida veterinary practices. We respect your patient relationships, return cases to your care, and communicate every step. This page is for veterinary professionals only.

Call (954) 772-0420 Email Records
Patients returned to your practice Idexx Neo with electronic records transfer Direct phone access during operating hours On-site diagnostics, surgery, and pharmacy
Professional referral
Built on respect for your practice

Built on Respect for Your Practice.

The veterinary landscape in South Florida has changed. Corporate consolidation has shifted how many emergency hospitals approach referrals, with some practices treating referred patients as opportunities to capture, not relationships to honor. Pet Emergency Center has chosen a different path.

We are independently owned by Dr. Seth Kiser and Paige Obrig, and we have served Fort Lauderdale and the surrounding communities for over 40 years. Our position has always been clear: when a daytime veterinarian sends a patient to us, we treat the referral relationship as an integral part of our work. We support your care. We do not compete with it.

Your patient comes back to you. That is not a courtesy. That is how we practice.

Pre-transfer communication

Pre-Transfer Communication.

Our veterinarians appreciate the opportunity to review the case and communicate with the referring veterinarian prior to transfer. A brief call ahead of arrival ensures a smooth transition for your patient and your client, and it lets our team prepare so the patient is treated without delay.

When you have a case headed our way, please call (954) 772-0420 to give us a heads-up. We will discuss the patient's condition, expected arrival time, and any clinical context that will help us pick up where you left off.

Documents Needed for Patient Referral.

To support continuity of care, please send the following with the referral or as soon as possible after:

  • Patient medical records
  • Medication dosage and time administered
  • Blood work results
  • Radiographs
  • Any additional test results relevant to the case

Documents can be sent by email or fax. Even a few key details ahead of arrival can save valuable time when the patient walks in.

Referral coordination

All Contact Info, In One Place.

All contact information for referral coordination, in one place.

After-hours direct line

Paige Obrig

For questions outside regular business hours when the main line is not the right path.

Communication back to your practice

Communication Back to Your Practice.

Our practice management software, Idexx Neo, fully integrates with our in-house lab and diagnostic imaging equipment. Patient records are sent electronically to your practice email. We communicate the case summary, diagnostic findings, treatments performed, medications dispensed, and discharge recommendations.

If you have not received records you are expecting, or if you need an update on a patient's condition, please reach out at any time.

On-site capabilities

On-Site Clinical Capabilities.

When you refer a patient, you can be confident that diagnostics, treatment, and stabilization can happen in the same visit, without additional transfers.

Emergency & Critical Care

Triage, stabilization, IV therapy, oxygen support, and continuous monitoring for hospitalized patients.

Learn more →

Emergency Surgery

On-site surgical suite with experienced surgical team. Foreign body, GDV, splenectomy, C-section, pyometra, wound repair, and other urgent procedures.

Learn more →

Diagnostics

In-house bloodwork, digital radiology, ultrasound, cytology, and urinalysis with same-visit results, fully integrated with Idexx Neo.

Learn more →

Pain Management

Multi-modal pain protocols tailored to species, age, and condition.

Learn more →

Pharmacy

Comprehensive in-house formulary, dispensed same-visit. Medications follow the patient home with clear instructions.

Learn more →

Telemedicine

Florida statewide telehealth consultations available for triage support.

Learn more →

Patients returned for follow-up

Patients Returned for Follow-Up.

Unless circumstances require otherwise, we return your patient to you for ongoing care. Suture removal, recheck exams, ongoing medication management, and post-emergency follow-up are best handled by the practice that knows the patient. We make that handoff explicit in every discharge.

For cases that require specialty referral beyond our scope, we coordinate transfer to the appropriate specialist and keep your practice informed throughout. You will not be left guessing where your patient went or what happened next.

FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions.

Specific to referring veterinarians. For other questions, call (954) 772-0420.

Compassionate care. Always here.

Thank You for Your Continued Support.

We are honored to be a referral partner for South Florida's daytime veterinary practices. If you have a case headed our way or would like to discuss how we work, call us anytime during operating hours.
